cross_file_consistency.reason: CP_AGENTS set was updated to add push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst. These three new agent names are now consistently registered in CP_AGENTS and DEV_REGISTRY_AGENTS (same set by reference). Consistent with their new agent .md files and tool-policy roles.

Three new role entries added: push-analyst (allowed_tools: Read/Glob/Grep/Bash/Write, write-scoped to /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/*), merge-analyst (same scope to /tmp/agentic-commit/merge-analyst/*), pull-analyst (allowed_tools: Read/Glob/Grep/Bash only — no Write, empty allowed_write_path_prefixes). Consistent with agent .md files: pull-analyst explicitly states 'Never write any file — this agent has no Write tool access'. Policy is correctly differentiated.
cross_file_consistency.verdict: roles are correctly scoped and differentiated

cross_file_consistency.reason: Agentic dispatch protocol (Steps 0-5) added. Previous version instructed direct push.sh call; new version gates on push-analyst grant validation before push.sh is invoked. Grant validation logic (nonce, branch, head_sha, remote_name, session_id, verdict, risks, expires_at fields) aligns with grant schema written by agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7.
cross_file_consistency.verdict: command protocol and agent grant schema are consistent

cross_file_consistency.reason: Agentic dispatch protocol (Steps 0-5) added. Grant validation fields (nonce, branch, source_tip, default_tip, default_branch, session_id, verdict, risks, expires_at) align with agents/merge-analyst.md Phase 8 grant schema. Expiry revalidation on mismatch (re-dispatch with fresh REQUEST_ID) aligns with merge-analyst 60s expiry design.
cross_file_consistency.verdict: command protocol and agent grant schema are consistent

cross_file_consistency.reason: Post-pull analyst dispatch protocol added (Steps 0-6). pull-analyst is advisory only — no grant, no blocking. Dispatch only for pull_exit_phase=success or stash_restoration_failed (not rebase_conflict). Consistent with agents/pull-analyst.md which declares 'You write no grant and block nothing' and 'Never write any file'.
cross_file_consistency.verdict: command protocol matches agent advisory-only contract

policy_gap_observations.reason: pull-analyst role has an empty allowed_write_path_prefixes list and no Write tool. This is intentional per rationale: 'pull-analyst writes no grant and blocks nothing'. However, the role also lacks the Glob tool that push-analyst and merge-analyst have. Checking agents/pull-analyst.md Workflow: it uses git log, git diff, git show — all via Bash. Glob is not needed for pull-analyst. No gap.
policy_gap_observations.verdict: omission of Glob is intentional; workflow is Bash-only for git commands

policy_gap_observations.reason: push-analyst and merge-analyst roles do not include /tmp/* in allowed_write_path_prefixes (only /tmp/agentic-commit/<role>/* is allowed). This is correct and tighter than changelog-analyst which also lacks broad /tmp/*. The audit log write destination /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/audit/<REPO_HASH>.log falls within /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/* so it is covered.
policy_gap_observations.verdict: write scope is correct and appropriately narrow
